title: larix vulgaris
intro: Larix vulgaris, also known as the common larch, is a deciduous coniferous tree native to northern and central Europe, northern Asia, and the mountains of North Africa. The bark and needles of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Larix vulgaris include reducing respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. The herbal preparations of Larix vulgaris include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory problems.
